conjecture_statement: Let $\\mathcal{P}_n$ be the set of all periodic sphere packings in $\\mathbb{R}^n$, and let $\\Delta_n = \\sup_{P \\in \\mathcal{P}_n} \\text{density}(P)$ be the maximum possible density. Let $\\Lambda_9 \\subset \\mathbb{R}^9$ be the optimal lattice for sphere packing in 9 dimensions (conjecturally, the Coxeter-Todd lattice). import Mathlib.Geometry.Euclidean.SpherePacking.Basic
import Mathlib.Analysis.InnerProductSpace.PiL2
import Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.Matrix.Gram

open Set Metric

/- We model R^n as `EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin n)` -/

/-- A predicate asserting that a sphere packing `P` in `n+1` dimensions is a
    periodic stacking of a layer packing `P_layer` in `n` dimensions. -/
